# Business Requirements Document: <project name>

| | |
|---|---|
| Status | Draft / In review / Approved |
| Owner | |
| Date | |
| Reviewers | |

## 1. Problem statement

Who has what problem, how painful is it, and how do they solve it today?
Two paragraphs max, no technology words.

## 2. Business objectives

Numbered, each with a measurable target and a date.
Example: "O1: $2k MRR from 5 paying SMB customers by 2026-12-01."

## 3. Why AI (and the non-AI baseline)

What would the best non-AI solution look like? Why is it insufficient?
If this section is unconvincing, stop here.

## 4. Acceptable error analysis

- What happens when the system is wrong? Who is affected and how badly?
- What error rate is tolerable per capability?
- What human oversight is required (review gates, approvals, audit trail)?

## 5. Stakeholders

| Role | Person | Involvement |
|---|---|---|
| Sponsor / decision-maker | | |
| Builder | | |
| Target users | | |
| Affected third parties | | |

## 6. Scope

**In scope (v1):**

**Explicitly out of scope:**

## 7. Constraints

Budget (build + monthly run), timeline, regulatory (CAN-SPAM, GDPR/CCPA if applicable),
brand/reputation, dependencies on third parties.

## 8. Success metrics

| Metric | Baseline | 90-day target | How measured |
|---|---|---|---|

## 9. Risks and assumptions

| # | Risk / assumption | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|---|

## 10. Cost model (rough)

Build effort estimate, monthly run cost at expected usage, cost per user/query,
break-even point against objectives in section 2.

## 11. Gate decision

Date, decision (go / no-go / revise), decided by, and one line of rationale.
